steps.observed: HTTP 200, response contains choices[0].logprobs with tokens, token_logprobs, top_logprobs, text_offset

steps.expected: HTTP 200, logprobs present, same behavior as logprobs: true
steps.observed: HTTP 200, logprobs data present with identical structure to logprobs: true
Both return legacy format with tokens, token_logprobs, top_logprobs, text_offset. Tokens=['L','yon','.','<|im_end|>'] in both cases (model chose same completion)

assertions.title: top_logprobs parameter controls number of alternatives returned

steps.expected: Up to 1 entry per position in top_logprobs
steps.observed: 1 entry per position (except null for first token)
Up to 5 entries per position (pos 1 had 5, pos 2 had 3)
HTTP 200 with valid response — negative value NOT rejected

assertions.issues: top_logprobs: -1 returns HTTP 200 instead of expected HTTP 400. Server does not validate negative top_logprobs values. Positive values (0, 1, 5) work correctly.

assertions.title: Non-streaming response includes logprobs in choices[0]

steps.action: POST /v1/completions with logprobs: true, stream: false, top_logprobs: 3
steps.observed: HTTP 200, choices[0].logprobs is dict with keys: tokens, token_logprobs, top_logprobs, text_offset

steps.action: Verify logprobs is not null or absent
steps.observed: choices[0].logprobs is a populated dict — legacy format confirmed

steps.action: POST /v1/completions with stream: true, logprobs: true, top_logprobs: 3
steps.expected: SSE stream with logprobs in every non-terminal chunk
steps.observed: SSE stream captured with 7 non-terminal chunks + [DONE]. Logprobs present in 3 chunks, absent in 4 text-only chunks. All token logprobs are present in the stream.

steps.expected: Token string, logprob, and top alternatives
steps.observed: Each logprobs chunk contains tokens array, token_logprobs array, top_logprobs array with multiple entries

steps.action: Check for top_logprobs with multiple entries
steps.expected: At least one chunk with >1 top_logprobs entry
steps.observed: 3 chunks have top_logprobs with multiple entries (3, 3, and 2 entries respectively)

evidence.note: Logprobs are sent in dedicated interleaved chunks (separate from text chunks), not in every chunk. All token logprobs are present in the stream. Pattern: text chunk -> logprobs chunk -> text chunk -> logprobs chunk.

assertions.title: Logprobs entries have token, logprob, top_logprobs fields

steps.action: Generate 3 completions with max_tokens=30, logprobs=true, top_logprobs=5
Validate each entry has required fields with correct types
steps.expected: token: non-empty string, logprob: negative float or null, top_logprobs: dict with token->logprob
steps.observed: All 93 positions have valid fields. All tokens are non-empty strings. All non-null logprobs are negative floats. All top_logprobs entries are dicts mapping string tokens to negative float logprobs.

steps.action: Check logprob values are negative or zero
steps.observed: All non-null logprobs are negative floats. No positive values found.

assertions.title: top_logprobs sorted descending by logprob

steps.action: Extract top_logprobs arrays from 3 requests (93 total positions, 90 with multi-entry top_logprobs)
steps.expected: Each top_logprobs array sorted in strictly descending order by logprob
steps.observed: Only 1 out of 90 multi-entry positions is correctly sorted. 89 out of 90 are NOT in descending order.

steps.action: Verify chosen token is first entry
steps.expected: Chosen token appears as first entry in top_logprobs
steps.observed: Chosen token is first in only 14 out of 90 positions. In 76 out of 90 positions, the chosen token is NOT the first entry.
e.g., [-2.48, -3.29, -3.23, -2.92, -1.10] — values are in arbitrary order, not sorted

assertions.issues: top_logprobs entries are NOT sorted in descending order by logprob. The entries appear to be in arbitrary/random order. Additionally, the chosen token is NOT guaranteed to be the first entry. This violates OpenAI API compatibility where top_logprobs must be sorted highest-probability-first with the chosen token first.

frictions.description: Streaming SSE sends logprobs in separate interleaved chunks rather than including logprobs in every data: line. A client that only checks the first chunk may miss logprobs. This is an implementation choice that deviates from the strict wording of the spec ("each SSE data: line MUST contain a logprobs field") but is functionally complete.
frictions.resolution: All logprobs data is present in the stream — just in dedicated chunks rather than every chunk. A client that processes all chunks will have complete logprobs data.

summary: Tested 7 assertions: 5 passed, 2 failed. VAL-COMP-003 fails because top_logprobs: -1 (negative) returns HTTP 200 instead of 400. VAL-COMP-007 fails because top_logprobs entries are not sorted in descending order by logprob (89/90 positions unsorted) and the chosen token is not guaranteed to be first (76/90 positions have chosen token not first).
